seo.title: 100 AI Content Generation resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical AI Content Generation resources for engineers.

content.intro: Building high-performance AI content generation pipelines requires moving beyond simple prompt-response cycles. This resource focuses on the technical infrastructure, validation frameworks, and programmatic scaling techniques necessary to deploy production-grade content systems using modern LLMs and web frameworks.

items.title: Vercel AI SDK (useChat & useCompletion)
items.description: Utilize the React hooks for streaming LLM responses directly into your frontend, reducing perceived latency for editors and users.

items.title: Zod Schema Validation
items.description: Define strict TypeScript schemas to validate JSON outputs from LLMs, ensuring that generated content fits your database structure before ingestion.

items.title: Instructor (Python/JS)
items.description: A library designed for structured data extraction; use it to force LLMs to return valid Pydantic or Zod objects for programmatic SEO metadata.

items.title: OpenAI Batch API
items.description: Use this for non-urgent content generation tasks to reduce costs by 50% compared to standard synchronous requests.

items.title: Astro Content Collections
items.description: Manage AI-generated Markdown or MDX files with built-in type safety and frontmatter validation for static site generation.

items.title: LangChain Expression Language (LCEL)
items.description: Compose modular chains for complex content workflows, such as multi-step research, drafting, and proofreading cycles.

items.title: Contentful Management API
items.description: Automate the creation and updating of content entries directly from your generation scripts, bypassing the web UI.

items.title: Sanity.io Webhooks
items.description: Trigger AI-driven SEO description generation or image alt-text creation whenever a new document is published in the CMS.

items.title: Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet
items.description: Leverage this model for long-form content that requires a more 'human' tone and superior adherence to complex formatting instructions.

items.title: Pinecone Vector Database
items.description: Store your existing high-performing articles as embeddings to provide the LLM with relevant context via RAG for brand voice consistency.

items.title: Self-Consistency Checking
items.description: Run the same prompt three times and use a secondary LLM call to compare outputs for factual discrepancies or hallucinations.

items.title: Perplexity API for Fact-Checking
items.description: Programmatically verify claims made in AI-generated drafts by querying the Perplexity online search API for real-time citations.

items.title: Textstat Library (Python)
items.description: Integrate Flesch-Kincaid readability scoring into your validation pipeline to ensure content remains at the target grade level.

items.title: Retool for HITL Workflows
items.description: Build a simple internal dashboard for editors to approve, reject, or edit AI-generated drafts before they hit the production database.

items.title: DeepL API Integration
items.description: Use DeepL for high-accuracy translation of generated content instead of relying solely on LLMs for multi-language support.

items.title: Copyscape Premium API
items.description: Automate plagiarism checks for every generated article to ensure the LLM hasn't inadvertently reproduced training data verbatim.

items.title: Few-Shot Prompting Templates
items.description: Maintain a library of successful 'Human-Written' examples in your system prompts to guide the LLM's stylistic choices.

items.title: LLM-as-a-Judge (Promptfoo)
items.description: Use the Promptfoo CLI to run test cases against your prompts and evaluate output quality using a stronger model like GPT-4o.

items.title: Azure Content Safety API
items.description: Filter generated text for hate speech, violence, or sensitive topics to protect your brand reputation at scale.

items.title: Pydantic Output Parsers
items.description: Utilize Pydantic's field validators to ensure that generated dates, URLs, and numbers fall within expected logical ranges.

items.title: Next.js Dynamic Routes
items.description: Generate thousands of landing pages using [slug].tsx templates populated by data from your AI generation pipeline.

items.title: Sitemap.js Automation
items.description: Script the dynamic updates of your sitemap.xml file to include new AI-generated pages as soon as they are published.

items.title: Satori for Dynamic OG Images
items.description: Automatically generate Open Graph images for every AI article using Vercel's Satori to improve social media click-through rates.

items.title: Upstash Redis for Prompt Caching
items.description: Cache responses for identical prompts to save on API costs and provide sub-millisecond response times for common queries.

items.title: GitHub Actions for Cron Publishing
items.description: Schedule a workflow to generate and commit one new article per day to your repository to maintain a fresh content signal.

items.title: DALL-E 3 Image API
items.description: Programmatically generate unique featured images for every post based on the article's summary to avoid stock photo repetition.

items.title: Internal Link Mapping Script
items.description: Build a Python script that scans new AI content and uses fuzzy matching to insert links to existing high-authority pages on your site.

items.title: PostHog A/B Testing
items.description: A/B test different AI-generated headlines or CTAs to identify which prompt variations lead to the highest user conversion.

items.title: Google Search Console API
items.description: Monitor the indexing status and performance of AI-generated pages to automatically flag and rework content that isn't ranking.

items.title: Turndown (HTML to Markdown)
items.description: Convert existing legacy content into Markdown to use as clean context for LLMs when generating updated or related versions.

pro_tips: Always use 'JSON Mode' or tool-calling features in LLM APIs to prevent preamble text like 'Here is your article' from breaking your parsers.
Implement a 'chain-of-thought' step where the LLM first creates an outline, and a second step where it writes the content based on that outline.
Include a 'negative prompt' section in your system instructions to explicitly list words or phrases the AI should avoid (e.g., 'delve', 'tapestry', 'in the digital age').
Use a vector database to store 'Human-Written Gold Standard' articles and inject the top 3 most relevant ones into the prompt as examples.
Never publish AI content without an automated check for broken links and 404s, especially when the AI suggests external resources.
